title: John Hope Bryant | Credit Score = Life Expectancy
summary: John Hope Bryant breaks down the undeniable link between credit scores, health, education, and wealth in America. From Chicago to Mississippi, he reveals the shocking connection between money, health, and opportunity — showing how a 700+ credit score neighborhood lives 20 years longer than a 580 score neighborhood. But he doesn’t stop at the problem — he shares the solutions that are already working, from children’s savings accounts to Operation HOPE’s financial coaching. Source: John Hope Bryant Speech, The Business Plan for Cleveland: The Untold Story of How We ALL Succeed  Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Jawanza Kunjufu | How to Close the Achievement Gap
summary: Dr. Jawanza Kunjufu delivers a challenging message on parenting, time, and academic achievement. Warning that this could be the first generation of Black youth not to surpass the last, he breaks down the truth about study habits, distractions, and expectations. This episode is a call to invest more time in our children—and raise the bar for their future. Source: Dr. Jawanza Kunjufu: Raising Black Boys 2  Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Carla Harris | The Work Doesn’t Speak, But Relationships Holler
summary: In this powerful episode, Carla Harris drops game on building relationships, embracing authenticity, and leading with intention. Success isn’t accidental — it’s strategic. If you’re ready to rise, take risks, and step into your excellence, this conversation is for you. 📚 Book Club Enrollment is Open! Our featured book is The Confident Mind by Dr. Nate Zinsser. The discussion begins Wednesday, September 10th. Join a community of like-minded listeners who are committed to personal growth, deep conversations, and taking action on what they learn. Click here to join! Source: 2018 Catalyst Awards Conference: Carla Harris Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Claud Anderson | No Ownership, No Power
summary: Without businesses, we own nothing. Without ownership, we have no power.” Dr. Claud Anderson lays it plain — jobs may maintain you, but only businesses create wealth and independence. In this eye-opening episode, he explains why entrepreneurship is the key to economic survival and Black empowerment. 📚 Book Club Enrollment is Open! Our featured book is The Confident Mind by Dr. Nate Zinsser. The discussion begins Wednesday, September 10th. Join a community of like-minded listeners who are committed to personal growth, deep conversations, and taking action on what they learn. Click here to join! Source: Dr Claud Anderson Black Dollars Making Sense Full Video with Q &A Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Dennis Kimbro | Success Always Leaves Footprints
summary: In this inspiring episode, Dr. Dennis Kimbro challenges us to live with vision and intention. He breaks down why self-talk shapes destiny, why goals must be written and lived daily, and why success always leaves footprints. This one is a masterclass in Black excellence, discipline, and belief.   Source: Think and Grow Rich A Black Choice Dr Dennis Kimbro Pt 4  Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Olympia LePoint | Turn Fear Into Fuel
summary: Rocket scientist Olympia LePoint shares how she conquered fear, rebuilt her confidence, and rewired her brain for success. She reveals the science of creating new neural pathways by taking action in direct opposition to fear — proving you can do anything you set your mind to. Source: Reprogramming your brain to overcome fear: Olympia LePoint at TEDxPCC Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Les Brown | You Gotta Be Hungry
summary: Les Brown shares his unforgettable journey from rejected radio hopeful to on-air personality — all powered by relentless hunger and service beyond expectation. In this inspiring talk, he reveals how persistence, preparation, and building relationships open doors, and why those who are truly hungry are unstoppable in creating the life they dream of. Source: 2009 Business & Finance MLK Convocation – Les Brown – 01/19/09 Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Gloria Mayfield Banks | The Three C’s of Success
summary: Dr. Gloria Mayfield-Banks reveals her proven “three C’s” formula for success: courage, confidence, and choices. In this inspiring talk, she shares her personal journey of overcoming dyslexia, pushing through fear, and building the confidence that creates life-changing opportunities. Learn how small, courageous decisions lead to big victories. Source: How to Change Your Life | Dr. Gloria Mayfield Banks Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Eric Thomas | Be Phenomenal or Be Forgotten
summary: Eric Thomas challenges you to pick a side: success or average. They can’t coexist. He breaks down why average destroys legacies, costs lives, and sets the wrong example — and why you must press to be phenomenal in every area of your life. Source: Eric Thomas - How To AVOID Being Average! (Motivational Keynote) Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Lisa Nichols | Your Dream Is Bigger Than You
summary: Lisa Nichols takes us behind the scenes of her “overnight success” — from reciting speeches to her grandmother, to serving people with no expectation of return, to finally making the phone ring. This is a masterclass in holding your dream full-term and doing the work no one sees. Source: Questions that Will Stir Your Soul | Lisa Nichols Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Rev. Ike | Stop Being Afraid to Prosper
summary: Rev. Ike delivers a message that’ll flip the script on how you see God and money. He says it’s time to throw out the poverty complex, embrace abundance, and live life more abundantly — the way the Bible intended. Source: Why Survival Mode is Blocking Your Abundance - Total Prosperity, Total Well Being, Part 2 Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: George Subira | From Anti-Capitalist to Economic Power
summary: George Subira dismantles the myths and mental blocks holding back Black economic power. He says it’s time to remember our entrepreneurs, produce and trade like every other thriving people, and stop acting like working for someone else is our destiny. Source: George Subira | Close Encounters of 4th Kind, Black Eco Development (29Dec97) Trenton Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Nicole LaBeach | Your Vision Is Waiting on You
summary: Dr. Nicole LaBeach redefines what it means to have vision. She says it’s not about what’s possible on paper — it’s about what you can see and are willing to say yes to. Own your vision, feed it, and watch it come alive. Source: Dr. Nicole LaBeach: Positioning to Win Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. George Fraser | Success Runs in Our Race
summary: Dr. George C. Fraser delivers a straight-talk lesson on money, unity, and economic power. With $1.1 trillion in buying power, he says the only thing we lack is each other — and it’s time to change that. Source: Rise or Die: Dev a New Consciousness & Habits for Self-Mastery Part 1 - Dr. George Fraser Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Carla Harris | If You Don’t Ask, You Don’t Get
summary: Carla Harris shares the wisdom of a lifetime — from speaking up in the rooms you’ve earned, to taking bold risks, to knowing your worth and asking for it. If you’re ready to own your power and never dim your light, this one’s for you. Source: Carla Harris Speaks at the 2017 Pennsylvania Conference for Women Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: John Hope Bryant | From Po’ to Powerful
summary: John Hope Bryant shares how a crushed velvet suit, a banker in a red tie, and a $40 candy hustle changed his life — and how we can shift our mindset from poverty to purpose. We never got the memo on wealth. But now it’s time we do. Source: John Hope Bryant Closing Remarks at the Hope Global Forum 2017 Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Mellody Hobson | No One Is Coming
summary: Mellody Hobson pulls no punches in this powerful talk on money, retirement, and financial responsibility. She tackles our fears, busts financial fairytales, and reminds us that no one is coming. From her personal story to national realities, this episode is a wake-up call — and a call to action. Source: Mellody Hobson, Ariel Investments, Discusses the Need for Financial Literacy Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Marina Perez | How to Read Your Credit Report Like a Pro
summary: Credit expert Marina Perez breaks down everything you thought you knew about credit. From hard inquiries to FICO scores to why your job application might pull your report — this one’s packed with the kind of knowledge that builds financial power. Source: Marina Perez: Credit - Your Key to all Success | Lunch & Learn Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Inky Johnson | Opposition Isn’t the End
summary: Inky Johnson delivers a fiery message about resilience, fatherhood, and what it means to “go heavy” in the face of adversity. With a powerful story about teaching his son to get back up — even bleeding — Inky reminds us that our purpose doesn’t quit when life gets hard. It’s time to go heavy. Source: Inky Johnson speaks to Broad River Retail Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Shelby Steele | The Danger of Dependence
summary: Author and scholar Shelby Steele delivers a thought-provoking message on why it's time to move beyond grievance and into greatness. He challenges us to stop living in protest and start living in good faith, where freedom and personal responsibility pave the way to real Black excellence. Source: Shelby Steele | Is White Guilt Destroying the Promise of Civil Rights? Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Kamilah Stevenson | Generational Health Starts With You
summary: Dr. Kamilah Stevenson shares her gripping journey from Olympic hopeful to chronic illness — and how she reclaimed her health by changing her mindset and her meals. In this powerful talk, she urges us to stop eating like we’re dying and start living like we mean it. Health isn’t a goal — it’s the foundation of your purpose. Download my FREE ebook Source: Thinking Your Way Into Health | Kamilah Stevenson | TEDxWillowCreek Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Malcolm-Jamal Warner | The Strength in Vulnerability
summary: Late actor and poet Malcolm-Jamal Warner delivers a powerful reflection on masculinity, identity, and emotional honesty. With raw vulnerability, he dismantles stereotypes of Black men, revealing how embracing his truth became his superpower. A must-listen for anyone ready to show up fully and authentically. Source: Vulnerability is my superpower | Malcolm-Jamal Warner | TEDxManhattanBeach Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Myron Golden | Get So Good They Can’t Deny You

Source: How To Get So Good They Can’t Deny You! Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

From rejection to resilience, Keith reminds us that with faith, family, and the right mindset, anything is possible. title: Inky Johnson | Finish What You Start
summary: Today on Black Excellence Daily, Inky Johnson delivers a passionate message on commitment, perseverance, and living with purpose. From overcoming paralysis to earning his master's degree, Inky reminds us that success isn’t about the outcome — it’s about honoring the process and those who invested in us. A must-listen on grit, legacy, and excellence. Source: Unavailable Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Eric Thomas | Producer or Consumer?
summary: In this fire-filled episode, Eric Thomas (ET the Hip Hop Preacher) delivers raw truth about why most people aren’t ready for the success they claim to want. From poverty mindsets to poor financial habits, ET breaks down the critical difference between being a consumer and becoming a producer. It’s not about what you have—it’s about how you think. Change your mindset, and wealth will chase you. Source: Eric Thomas | Producer vs Consumer (Eric Thomas Motivation) Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Lisa Nichols | Dr. King Had Fear — So Can You
summary: Lisa Nichols delivers a soul-stirring message inspired by Dr. Martin Luther King's lesser-known letter. She reminds us that greatness includes fear, doubt, and humanity. This powerful episode calls you to embrace your imperfections, stand in your truth, and stretch into your purpose — because your dash matters, and your life is meant to disrupt, inspire, and transform. Source: What Are You Willing To Do To Achieve Your Dream? | Lisa Nichols Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Myles Munroe | Freedom Begins with Truth
summary: Dr. Myles Munroe breaks down the link between ignorance and bondage, the difference between knowledge and truth, and why embracing divine truth is the path to freedom. Prepare to unlearn, relearn, and unlock your full potential. Source: Dr. Myles Munroe || The Best Kept Secret || Unused Power || Hidden Potential Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: George Subira | Capitalism, Contradictions, and the Culture
summary: In this compelling episode, George Subira challenges the Black community’s relationship with business, capitalism, and legacy. From forgotten Black entrepreneurs to the contradictions of economic activism, he delivers a passionate call to reframe how we view wealth, power, and self-determination. Source: George Subira | Close Encounters of 4th Kind, Black Eco Development (29Dec97) Trenton Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

summary: In this eye-opening episode, Mellody Hobson confronts the national crisis of financial illiteracy. From the streets of Chicago to systemic disparities in wealth and access, she explores why financial education is missing in our schools and how it impacts the Black community. A must-hear on the urgent need to teach America how to "drive" its financial future. Source: BBSA Conference Mellody Hobson Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Myron Golden | How to Make Books Make You Millions
summary: Myron Golden breaks down how to master the books you read—not just skim for knowledge, but extract wisdom that transforms. Discover his powerful note-taking method, mindset on mastery over memorization, and how books have generated millions by how he engages with them. This isn’t just reading—it’s a strategy for elevation. Source: How To Get More Out Of The Books You Read Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Kelvin Boston | How to Close The Wealth Gap
summary: Financial expert Kelvin Boston breaks down the true meaning of net worth and why income alone isn't enough. He shares a blueprint for building generational wealth through homeownership, investments, and financial planning. This episode is a wake-up call for Black families to stop playing no-win monopoly—and start owning their financial futures. Website: None Source: Kelvin Boston Increasing Net Worth | American Black Journal Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Les Brown | Dying to Go to Work: Break Free & Find Your Purpose
summary: Are you living your life's true purpose, or simply going through the motions? In this powerful episode, Les Brown challenges us to confront the fears, doubts, and limiting beliefs that keep us stuck. With compelling personal stories and inspiring insight, he urges us to discover our purpose, invest in ourselves, and take even the smallest step toward our dreams. A must-listen for anyone ready to rise above fear and pursue greatness. Source: Unknown Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Shelby Steele | The Trauma of Freedom
summary: What happens when freedom arrives — but we’re unprepared for it? In this thought-provoking episode, Dr. Shelby Steele explores how Black America faced the trauma of freedom after the Civil Rights Movement, the rise of bad faith identity, and why the real revolution lies in cultural capital, personal agency, and thriving beyond white guilt. Source: Shelby Steele | Is White Guilt Destroying the Promise of Civil Rights? Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Myles Munroe | The Wealthiest Place on Earth
summary: The wealthiest place on Earth isn’t where you think—it’s the graveyard. In this powerful message, Dr. Myles Munroe challenges you to die empty by releasing your gifts, dreams, and potential into the world. Don’t let the cemetery claim your unwritten books or unfulfilled purpose. Pour it all out. Leave nothing behind. Source: The Wealthy Place Cemetery - Dr. Myles Munroe Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Myron Golden | Familiarity Breeds Failure
summary: Myron Golden brings the heat on why familiarity breeds failure. If you’re stuck in old habits, old places, and old thinking, you're blocking your blessings. Learn why you must challenge familiar mindsets, embrace new ideas, and grow beyond the struggle—because your next level demands a new you. Source: Stop Floundering In The Land Of The Familiar Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Bishop T.D. Jakes | You Are Enough — Flaws and All
summary: Bishop T.D. Jakes delivers a powerful message on authenticity in a filtered world. From the dangers of comparison to the beauty of imperfections, this episode is a soulful reminder that your flaws are proof of your authenticity. Love your real self — because you are enough, spots, scars, and all. Source: Ron Carpenter Facebook Live Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Farrah Gray | He Made Millions by Age 14: Dr. Farrah Gray’s Blueprint
summary: Dr. Farrah Gray made his first million at 14 and earned a doctorate by 21. In this episode, he breaks down how to unlock your God-given purpose with three powerful questions. From dreaming big to giving back, Dr. Gray challenges you to stop settling for bones and go after the steak—your true calling. Vision over fear. Purpose over paycheck. Source: How to Get Rich Without College Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Eric Thomas | Scroll Less, Build More
summary: Eric Thomas drops powerful wisdom on how the world’s most successful people master one key habit: a consistent routine. From outsmarting distractions to learning the language of success, ET shares how discipline, mindset shifts, and self-accountability took him from struggle to significance. Don’t miss this wake-up call. Source: Eric Thomas | Success Routine (Eric Thomas Motivation) Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Steve Harvey | Stop Playing It Safe
summary: In this electrifying message, Steve Harvey urges us all to jump—to take the leap of faith toward living in our God-given gifts. He reminds us that true success doesn’t come from playing it safe, but from trusting that your parachute will open. This is your wake-up call to stop existing and start living. Source: Jump Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Robert Woodson | Teaching Promise, Not Just Pain
summary: Today, Robert Woodson challenges the narrative of victimhood with a powerful message of resilience, redemption, and historical truth. In this episode, he highlights untold stories of Black excellence, achievement under adversity, and the importance of teaching America’s promise — not just its flaws — to inspire the next generation. Source: NCLS 2022: Robert Woodson Keynote Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Inky Johnson | Nothing Can Stop an Unshakeable Purpose
summary: In this episode, Inky Johnson shares what it means to live with purpose beyond self. From life-altering adversity to daily acts of service, he reminds us that true greatness lies in who we become and what we give. A moving reflection on resilience, love, and the power of perspective — this is one you won’t forget. Source: Inky speaks at Catalyst Leadership Confrence Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. George C. Fraser | Why Wealth Matters: A Blueprint for Black Excellence
summary: Today, Dr. George C. Fraser breaks down what true leadership means — action, ownership, and influence rooted in responsibility and generational impact. In this powerful episode, he challenges us to rethink wealth, power, and legacy in the Black community. It’s not just about progress — it’s about purpose, power, and passing it on. Source: Dr George C Fraser Speaks at First Financial Security, Inc Leaders Convention Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Rev. Ike | Life Gives to the Giver, Takes from the Taker
summary: Reverend Ike delivers a masterclass on prosperity thinking, challenging the toxic mindset of ‘something for nothing.’ In this bold and spirited message, he shares timeless principles: life gives to the giver and takes from the taker. If you want to be blessed, you must invest — because the true gift of money begins with a giving mentality. Source: Recognizing the Givers and the Takers - Rev. Ike's The Power of Money Seminar, Part 10 Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Les Brown | Live Your Dreams, Not Your Fears
summary: In this electrifying message, Les Brown reminds us that we can either live our dreams — or our fears. Through humor, vulnerability, and powerful storytelling, he challenges us to confront what’s holding us back. Learn how to face fear head-on, embrace growth, and realize that the bulldogs chasing us often have no teeth. Source: Les Brown - Live Your Dreams (1991) Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Carla Harris | Your Reputation Is Your Resume
summary: Wall Street powerhouse Carla Harris drops her game-changing 'Perception Pearl' — a must-hear lesson on how perception shapes opportunity. Learn how to train others to see you as the leader you are by mastering three key adjectives that align with your authenticity and your organization’s values. Power. Clarity. Strategy. Source: How To Own Your Power Presentation - Carla Harris - Take The Lead Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: 146. 非認知能力を伝え続けるパッションの裏側
podcast_name: Deep Within 奥深くに存在する『本来のあなた』と深く繋がるスペース
summary: Seen. Heard. Paid  Speak with Clarity.｜明確に伝え、支払う準備ができたクライアントを惹きつける  You’re showing up. You’re doing the work.  あなたはもう、努力している。  毎日SNSに現れ、心を込めたコンテンツを届けている。  学びにも投資して、サービスも創り上げた。  それでもまだ、「本当に来てほしいお客様」からの問い合わせが来ない。  コンテンツに反応はあるのに、申込みにはつながらない。  なぜ？   その答えは、あなたの努力が足りないからではなく、メッセージの"伝わり方"にあるのです。  この3日間のマスタークラスで得られるもの： ✔️ 今のメッセージがなぜ届かないのか──その本当の原因と、見落としがちなポイントを明確にする  ✔️ あなたの専門性と想いが、なぜ伝わっていないのかを分析し、「お客様からの見え方」とのズレを解消する  ✔️ 「誰のための、どんな価値を持つサービスか？」を言語化し直すことで、  今すぐサポートを求めていて、お支払いする準備ができているお客様に自然と届くメッセージに変わる  ✔️ 自分の強み・方向性・伝えるべき言葉がはっきりし、ブレずに発信・販売していくための軸が整う  登録はこちら  ブータンのインスタグラムはここから

title: Vusi Thembekwayo | Entrepreneur? Business Owner? Self-Employed? Know the Difference.
summary: Entrepreneurship isn’t one-size-fits-all. In this powerful episode, Vusi Thembekwayo breaks down the difference between being self-employed, a professional, a business owner, and a true entrepreneur. Learn why growth can’t be skipped and how becoming ‘dangerous enough’ is key to building a scalable, sustainable business.” Source: “The 90 Day Test” for entrepreneurs Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Eric Thomas | No More Excuses: It’s Time to Level Up
summary: In this fiery wake-up call, Eric Thomas drops truth bombs about personal accountability and the mindset holding you back. You’re not stuck because of the system, the season, or other people — it’s you. From procrastination to poverty thinking, ET breaks down how to stop making excuses and start doing the work that unlocks your next level. Source: Eric Thomas - NO EXCUSES (Eric Thomas Motivation) - No Music Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Michael Colyar | Dream Big, Write It Down, Watch It Happen
summary: Comedian and motivational speaker Michael Colyar breaks down the real power behind vision boards—and why everyone striving for success should have one. In this energetic episode, he shares personal stories, practical tips, and a dose of humor to show how clarity, belief, and bold dreams can shape your reality. Source: Michael Colyar - Making a Difference and Keeping it Real! | 2016 Empower Series Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: 145. マーケティングを正しく行うことでセールスがシンプルになる
podcast_name: Deep Within 奥深くに存在する『本来のあなた』と深く繋がるスペース
summary: 5月のマスタークラスは終了いたしました。ご参加いただいた方はありがとうございました。  このエピソードはレッスンの一部を紹介しています。次回のお知らせをお待ちください。  Lesson 2: シンプルに売る: 自分のHuman Designをセールスに活かす  セールスは説得ではなく、共鳴と信頼の問題です。このレッスンでは、自分のヒューマンデザインに合わせたシンプルで本物のメッセージを活用し、無理なく魅力的なセールスを行う方法を学びます。自分らしい方法で、自然にクライアントとの信頼関係を築いていきます。

title: Dr. Dennis Kimbro | Mediocrity is Not Allowed
summary: Dr. Dennis Kimbro delivers a masterclass on grit, excellence, and the power of persistence. From Bill Gates to Black entrepreneurs like Bob Johnson and Greg Barranco, he shares real stories that prove success isn’t magic — it’s statistical. If you dare to lead, work hard, and never compromise your values, the marketplace will find you. Source: Unlocking Your Full Potential with Dr. Dennis Kimbro Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Jawanza Kunjufu | Smart ≠ Acting White: Reclaiming Black Excellence
summary: Dr. Jawanza Kunjufu breaks down the power of the peer group in shaping Black academic achievement. He explores why schools must be seen as social institutions, how peer pressure affects Black boys, and why integrating role models, academic competition, and cultural pride is critical for real educational equity. Insightful, urgent, and empowering. Source: Developing Positive Self-Images In Black Children (1988) | Dr. Jawanza Kunjufu Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: 144.売れる方じゃなくて自分の本質で届けたい by Miwa
podcast_name: Deep Within 奥深くに存在する『本来のあなた』と深く繋がるスペース
summary: SistersのMiwaさんのインタビュー  ニュースレター💌　こちら  インスタグラム　こちら     「他のプログラムとは全く違う」   表面的なノウハウではたどり着けない、深い自己理解と本質からのビジネス構築。      いろんなプログラムを受けてきたけれど、エミさんのプログラムは深さが違う。ネット検索や手軽なワークショップでは決して触れられない、自分の根っこと繋がる時間だった。     ✔ 売れる“型”に自分を合わせて苦しかった日々  ✔ 成果は出ても、続けられなかった違和感  ✔ 「もっと私らしいやり方があるはず」   そんな葛藤を経て、Miwaさんがたどり着いたのは、「自分の特性を活かして、自然体のまま届けられるブランド」でした。     「自分のやり方でいいんだ」と初めて思えた   「ヒューマンデザインで自分を深く理解し、それを信じてくれる人がいたからこそ、“私のままでいい”と確信できたんです。」     エミのプログラムでは、テンプレートを押し付けることはしません。   代わりに、あなたのエネルギーの流れ・強み・届けたい願いに合わせて、ビジネスの形を一緒に紡いでいきます。     特徴的なサポート内容  「売れる型」ではなく、「あなた自身の本質」から組み立てるブランド設計  ヒューマンデザイン×ビジネス視点での個別アプローチ   リンクノード、Calling、ソウルクライアントといった魂から響く切り口   思考ではなく感覚」に寄り添う予定の時間を超えてでも向き合ってくれる丁寧な伴走      🔥「本当は、花火をあげたいのにあげられなかった」   Miwaさんが言葉にした葛藤は、多くの女性起業家の心にも響くはずです。      もし、売れるために頑張ってきたけれど、自分がどんどん見えなくなってしまったと感じているなら。   あなたにも、「本当の自分」に還って、ビジネスを再構築するタイミングがきているのかもしれません。

title: Lisa Nichols | You Were Built From the Best
summary: Lisa Nichols shares a powerful story from her teenage years as a competitive swimmer who always came in last — until one life-changing conversation with her grandmother rewired her mindset. In this heartfelt message, she reminds us that “quitters never win and winners never quit,” and that the real shift happens not in the world around us, but in the words we speak to ourselves. Source: How to truly MOVE your audience | Lisa Nichols Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Myron Golden | Say No, Get Rich: Crush Laziness, Kill Ignorance, and Build Unstoppable Wealth
summary: Financial freedom starts with saying no — to laziness, ignorance, and financial frivolity. In this powerful talk, Myron Golden shares how diligence, wisdom, and the right mindset are key to transforming your finances. Discover why working hard for money is a trap—and how to start working hard on yourself instead. Source: Learn To Love “NO”!  Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Rev. Ike | Success is a State of Mind: How to Kill Excuses and Rise with Unstoppable Belief
summary: Reverend Ike delivers a fiery sermon on the power of the mind to transcend poverty and oppression. In this uncompromising message, he urges listeners to reject the “poverty self-image” and embrace success, not as a privilege, but as a birthright. "It’s not the color of your skin—it’s the color of your mind." Prepare to be challenged, inspired, and activated. Source: The Dangerous Thoughts that Lead to Your Success – Rev. Ike’s The Glory of the Cross, Part 2 Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram

title: Dr. Claud Anderson | The Five Floors to Black Power: A Blueprint for Wealth
summary: In this episode, Dr. Claud Anderson breaks down why Black wealth vanishes before it multiplies — and how group economics can change everything. From building real communities to owning the economy floor by floor, this is a no-fluff lesson in economic power, Black self-determination, and strategic survival. Source: [2014] Dr Claud Anderson -State of Black America Hosted by Sean Croxton Follow me on Instagram
